resources.string: Quick start guide
Skip
Back
Next
Done
%1$d of %2$d
Track income as it happens
Add income manually or import a supported bank statement when you want a faster starting point.
Use the date, source category, and notes to keep each declaration month clean.
Non-GEL income stays explicit instead of being silently converted.
Resolve GEL amounts with confidence
The app uses official NBG rates when they are available and keeps unresolved FX visible until you review it.
Resolve month FX from the month screen or add a manual FX override when needed.
Declaration totals only become fully ready after the needed FX is resolved.
Stay on top of monthly declarations
Each month shows readiness, filing and payment status, reminders, and local backup/export tools in Settings.
Use month status updates to move work from draft through filing and payment to settled.
If you need a refresher later, open Help & feedback in Settings.
Help & FAQ
How this app works
Short practical answers for the main product flows. The app is local-first, so avoid sharing taxpayer IDs, imported PDFs, or backup/export files in public feedback.
Quick actions
View quick start guide again
Rate this app
Send feedback
Feedback opens a public browser page. Anything you write can be visible to others, so do not include taxpayer IDs, PDFs, or backup/export files.
How do I add income?
Use Add income for manual entry, or Import statement for a supported bank PDF. Each entry needs a date, amount, currency, and source category, and you can edit entries later from the month screen.
How is GEL conversion calculated?
GEL income uses the original amount directly. For other currencies, the app uses official NBG rates when available or keeps the entry unresolved so totals are never silently guessed.
What if there is no FX rate for the date?
If no official rate is available for that date, the entry stays unresolved and the month remains not fully ready. You can retry official resolution later or set a manual FX override for that entry.
How do monthly declarations work?
Each income month maps to the next month’s filing window. The month screen shows Graph 20, cumulative Graph 15, estimated tax, zero-declaration guidance, and copy tools when the month is ready.
How do reminders and statuses work?
Reminder days and time are configured in Settings. Month statuses move from draft through filed and payment states to settled, and overdue is derived automatically from the due date.
When should I use backup or export?
Use CSV export when you want spreadsheet-style data outside the app. Use JSON backup when moving to a new device, before reinstalling, or when you want to restore the full local app state later.
Where can I leave feedback?
Open Send feedback from Settings or Help. It opens a public issue form in your browser with app version and device context prefilled. Keep tax IDs, PDFs, and backup/export files out of the report.
Could not open Google Play.
Could not open the feedback page.
